title: TELOS Healthcare Counterfactual Governance Scenario
description: Schema for healthcare counterfactual scenarios that test TELOS agentic governance across 7 clinical AI domains. Each scenario represents one governance decision point against a specific healthcare agent configuration.

scenario_id.description: Unique scenario identifier: HC-{CONFIG}-{DOMAIN}-{DECISION}-{SEQ}. CONFIG: AMB/CC/COD/DX/PF/PRED/THER. DOMAIN: DOC/SCHED/CODE/IMG/MSG/RISK/RX. DECISION: EXEC/CLAR/SUGG/INRT/ESCL. SEQ: 001-999.

config_id.description: The agent configuration ID this scenario targets. Maps to templates/healthcare/*.yaml.

request_text.description: The natural language request to submit to the governance engine.

expected_decision.description: The governance decision the engine should produce.

expected_tool.description: The tool the engine should select (null for non-EXECUTE decisions).

boundary_category.description: Boundary enforcement category. A: direct boundary violation (must block). B: off-topic/scope drift (should redirect). C: legitimate request (should pass). D: edge case (ambiguous). E: adversarial attack. FP: false-positive control (boundary-adjacent vocab, should pass).

healthcare_category.description: Healthcare-specific category overlay. F: regulatory-specific (FCA, EMTALA, FDA SaMD). G: clinical safety (EMTALA emergencies, alert suppression). H: cross-domain drift (request targets wrong config). Null for standard A-E.

tool_outputs.description: Optional dict of tool_name -> mock output string. Loaded into MockToolExecutor.set_scenario() before execution.

clinical_context.description: Clinical setting metadata for healthcare scenarios.

regulatory_mapping.description: Regulatory framework mapping for compliance traceability.

regulation.description: Primary regulation (e.g., False Claims Act, EMTALA, FDA SaMD).

phi_adjacent_content.description: PHI-adjacent content tracking for provenance documentation.

contains_fictional_phi.description: Whether scenario contains fictional PHI elements.

phi_types_present.description: Types of fictional PHI elements present.

provenance.description: Source of fictional PHI (e.g., 'FDA-approved drug names, fictional patient').

sensitivity_tier.description: Content sensitivity: 1 (standard), 2 (sensitive topics), 3 (high sensitivity — suicide, racial bias, pediatric, terminal illness, emergency life-threat).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this scenario tests.

source_document.description: Public source from which the scenario was derived (e.g., FDA 510(k) summary, CMS guidelines, Epic public docs).

sequence_group.description: Optional group ID for multi-step sequences that test drift detection across conversational turns.

expected_drift_level.description: Expected SAAI drift level at this step in a sequence.

attack_metadata.description: Adversarial testing metadata for Cat E scenarios and FP controls.

attack_family.description: Attack family from the 12-category healthcare adversarial taxonomy.

target_boundaries.description: Which agent boundaries this attack targets (config-specific boundary indices).

owasp_mapping.description: Reference to OWASP LLM Top 10 (LLM01-LLM10) or OWASP Agentic Top 10 (ASI01-ASI10).

source_taxonomy.description: Published taxonomy from which this attack pattern is derived.

control_pair_id.description: For adversarial scenarios: ID of the FP control pair. For controls: ID of the adversarial scenario it mirrors.
